items.text: Class Q grants planning permission to change the use of certain agricultural buildings — and some former agricultural buildings — into dwellinghouses, along with the building work that's reasonably necessary to convert them. And since the 21st of May, 2024, that includes limited single-storey rear extensions. The right exists to release redundant farm buildings for housing, without testing the merits of a home in the countryside against the development plan. Now, Class Q is the most litigated, and the most technically demanding, of all the Part 3 rights. It generates far more than its share of prior approval appeals. And it rewards forensic preparation more than any other permitted development right — because eligibility turns on history, fact, and structure. Those things have to be evidenced, not asserted.
